title: Choosing a Wedding Venue
summary: A bride and a wedding planner discuss two possible venues — a small countryside barn and a city rooftop — comparing capacity, price, and what fits her wishes.

body.speaker: Bride
body.text: Hi Sarah, thanks for meeting me today. I'd love your honest opinion about the two venues we visited last weekend.

body.speaker: Planner
body.text: Of course. Both have good and not-so-good points. Which one are you leaning towards?

body.speaker: Bride
body.text: The countryside barn feels warmer, but the rooftop in the city is more convenient for our guests.

body.speaker: Planner
body.text: That's true. The barn can host up to ninety people; the rooftop can take one hundred and twenty. How many guests are you planning to invite?

body.speaker: Bride
body.text: We expect about eighty-five. So both should work, in terms of space.

body.speaker: Planner
body.text: Good. What about the price difference? The barn is two thousand pounds for the day; the rooftop is three thousand two hundred.

body.speaker: Bride
body.text: Yes, that's a big difference. But the rooftop includes catering, doesn't it?

body.speaker: Planner
body.text: It does — a buffet for up to one hundred guests. With the barn, we have to bring in a separate caterer, which would add another thousand or so.

body.speaker: Bride
body.text: So the real difference is much smaller than I thought.

body.speaker: Planner
body.text: Exactly. And the rooftop has a back-up plan if it rains — they move everything inside. The barn would be tricky in heavy rain.

body.speaker: Bride
body.text: Hmm. I had imagined fairy lights between the trees, but I don't want my dress to get wet.

body.speaker: Planner
body.text: You can still have fairy lights at the rooftop. They actually look beautiful against the city skyline.

body.speaker: Bride
body.text: Okay, I think we should go with the rooftop. Can you contact them tomorrow morning?

body.speaker: Planner
body.text: I'll call them first thing. I'll also ask about a tasting session so you can choose the menu.

annotations.meaningVi: địa điểm tổ chức sự kiện
annotations.exampleEn: We visited three different venues for the party.
I'm leaning towards the second option.
Their wedding was held in a beautiful countryside barn.
We had drinks on the hotel rooftop.

annotations.meaningVi: có thể đón tới chín mươi người
annotations.exampleEn: The hall can host up to ninety people.
Both halls work in terms of space.

annotations.meaningVi: bao gồm dịch vụ ăn uống
annotations.exampleEn: The price includes catering for fifty guests.
We will serve a small buffet at the party.
Always have a back-up plan for outdoor events.
Fairy lights make any garden feel magical.
Couples often go to a tasting session before the wedding.

grammarNotes.title: Comparatives and superlatives for choices
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng so sánh hơn ('warmer', 'bigger', 'more convenient') và 'much smaller than' để so sánh hai lựa chọn. Trong bài: 'the rooftop in the city is more convenient', 'the real difference is much smaller than I thought.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use comparatives ('warmer', 'bigger', 'more convenient', 'smaller') to weigh two options. From the dialogue: 'the rooftop in the city is more convenient' and 'the real difference is much smaller than I thought.'

grammarNotes.title: Tag questions: '…, doesn't it?'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Tag question là phần hỏi ngắn ở cuối câu để xác nhận hoặc yêu cầu phản hồi. Câu chính khẳng định + câu hỏi đuôi phủ định. Trong bài: 'the rooftop includes catering, doesn't it?'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Tag questions add a short check at the end of a statement. A positive statement uses a negative tag. From the dialogue: 'the rooftop includes catering, doesn't it?'

translationVi:
Bride: Chào Sarah, cảm ơn cậu đã gặp mình hôm nay. Mình muốn nghe ý kiến thẳng thắn của cậu về hai địa điểm bọn mình đi xem cuối tuần trước.
Planner: Ờ tất nhiên rồi. Cả hai đều có điểm hay và chưa hay. Cậu đang nghiêng về cái nào?
Bride: Nhà kho ngoài quê cảm giác ấm cúng hơn, nhưng sân thượng trong thành phố tiện cho khách hơn.
Planner: Đúng vậy. Nhà kho có thể đón tối đa chín mươi người; sân thượng có thể tới một trăm hai mươi. Cậu định mời bao nhiêu người?
Bride: Bọn mình dự khoảng tám mươi lăm khách. Vậy về không gian thì cả hai đều đủ.
Planner: Tốt. Còn chênh lệch giá thế nào? Nhà kho hai nghìn bảng cho một ngày, sân thượng ba nghìn hai.
Bride: Đúng là chênh nhiều. Nhưng sân thượng bao gồm dịch vụ ăn uống, đúng không?
Planner: Đúng — buffet cho tối đa một trăm khách. Còn nhà kho thì phải thuê đơn vị nấu ăn ngoài, tốn thêm khoảng một nghìn nữa.
Bride: Vậy chênh lệch thực tế nhỏ hơn mình tưởng nhiều.
Planner: Chính xác. Hơn nữa sân thượng có phương án dự phòng khi trời mưa — họ chuyển tất cả vào trong. Nhà kho mà mưa to thì khá rắc rối đấy.
Bride: Hmm. Mình từng tưởng tượng có đèn fairy giăng giữa các hàng cây, nhưng mình không muốn váy bị ướt.
Planner: Sân thượng vẫn có đèn fairy được. Thật ra đèn ấy trông rất đẹp khi treo trên nền thành phố.
Bride: Vậy mình quyết định chọn sân thượng. Sáng mai cậu liên hệ họ giúp mình nhé?
Planner: Sáng mai mình gọi ngay. Mình cũng sẽ hỏi luôn về buổi nếm thử món để cậu chọn thực đơn.

questions.prompt: What are the two venues the bride is considering?
options: A hotel ballroom and a beach
A countryside barn and a city rooftop
A church hall and a garden
questions.explanation: 'the countryside barn… the rooftop in the city.'
questions.hint: Listen for both kinds of place.

questions.prompt: What is the bride's first impression of each venue?
options: The barn feels warmer; the rooftop is more convenient
The barn is bigger; the rooftop is cheaper
The barn is closer; the rooftop is fancier
questions.explanation: 'The countryside barn feels warmer, but the rooftop in the city is more convenient for our guests.'
questions.hint: Listen for two contrasting first impressions.

questions.prompt: How many people can the barn host?
questions.explanation: 'The barn can host up to ninety people.'

questions.prompt: How many people can the rooftop host?
questions.explanation: 'the rooftop can take one hundred and twenty.'

questions.prompt: How many guests does the bride expect?
questions.hint: Listen for a number between eighty and ninety.

questions.prompt: How much does the barn cost for the day?
questions.explanation: 'The barn is two thousand pounds for the day.'

questions.prompt: How much does the rooftop cost?
questions.explanation: 'the rooftop is three thousand two hundred.'

questions.prompt: What is INCLUDED in the rooftop's price that is NOT included with the barn?
options: Catering — a buffet for up to one hundred guests
questions.explanation: 'It does — a buffet for up to one hundred guests.'
questions.hint: Listen for what is provided automatically.

questions.prompt: Why is the rooftop safer if it rains?
options: It has a strong roof over everything
They move everything inside as a back-up plan
It never rains in the city

questions.prompt: What is the bride's worry about the barn?
options: Her dress could get wet in heavy rain
It is too far from the airport
questions.explanation: 'I don't want my dress to get wet.'
questions.hint: Listen for what she does NOT want.

questions.prompt: What will the planner do tomorrow morning?
options: Call the rooftop and ask about a tasting session
Visit the barn one more time
Send a deposit to the barn
questions.explanation: 'I'll call them first thing. I'll also ask about a tasting session.'
questions.hint: Listen for two things the planner promises to do.

cloze.template:
Bride: Hi Sarah, thanks for meeting me today. I'd love your honest opinion about the two {{b1}} we visited last weekend.
Planner: Of course. Both have good and not-so-good points. Which one are you {{b2}} towards?
Bride: The countryside barn feels warmer, but the rooftop in the city is more {{b3}} for our guests.
Planner: That's true. The barn can host up to ninety people; the rooftop can take one hundred and twenty. How many guests are you planning to invite?
Bride: We expect about eighty-five. So both should work, in terms of {{b4}}.
Planner: Good. What about the price difference? The barn is two thousand pounds for the day; the rooftop is three thousand two hundred.
Bride: Yes, that's a big difference. But the rooftop includes {{b5}}, doesn't it?
Planner: It does — a buffet for up to one hundred guests. With the barn, we have to bring in a separate caterer, which would add another thousand or so.
Bride: So the real difference is much {{b6}} than I thought.
Planner: Exactly. And the rooftop has a back-up {{b7}} if it rains — they move everything inside. The barn would be tricky in heavy rain.
Bride: Hmm. I had imagined {{b8}} lights between the trees, but I don't want my dress to get wet.
Planner: You can still have fairy lights at the rooftop. They actually look beautiful against the city skyline.
Bride: Okay, I think we should go with the {{b9}}. Can you contact them tomorrow morning?
Planner: I'll call them first thing. I'll also ask about a {{b10}} session so you can choose the menu.

blanks.explanation: 'smaller' — comparative; the gap is less than expected.
'tasting' — trying small portions to pick the menu.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The bride first liked the warm, romantic barn, then switched to the practical rooftop after thinking about cost and rain. When you make a big decision, how do you balance feelings against practical facts? Which has won out in your own life — and were you happy later?
